claimText: Trans-fatty acids (TFA) are unsaturated fatty acids that can be produced industrially via hydrogenation or found naturally in animal-source foods.
evidenceExcerpt: Trans-fatty acids (TFA) are unsaturated fatty acids produced industrially via hydrogenation or found naturally in animal-source foods.
examUse: Statement-based question on the chemical nature and sources of trans fats.
distractorHooks: Trans fats are saturated fatty acids
Trans fats are exclusively produced industrially and have no natural sources
Trans fats are naturally found in plant-source foods

claimText: Consumption of trans-fatty acids raises the level of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-c), which is known as bad cholesterol.
evidenceExcerpt: TFAs raise bad cholesterol (LDL-c) and are linked to inflammation, obesity, diabetes, and high blood pressure.
examUse: Statement-based question on the physiological and health impacts of trans fats.
distractorHooks: Trans fats raise high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol
Trans fats lower low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol

claimText: The REPLACE Action Package is an initiative by the World Health Organization (WHO) aimed at eliminating industrially produced trans fats globally.
evidenceExcerpt: WHO's REPLACE Action Package aims to eliminate industrially produced trans fats globally.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching global health initiatives to their specific objectives and parent organizations.
distractorHooks: REPLACE Action Package is an initiative by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO)
The initiative aims to eliminate saturated fats globally
The initiative targets the reduction of artificial sweeteners

claimText: India's Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) enforced a regulation in 2021 limiting trans fats in oils to no more than 2%.
evidenceExcerpt: India's FSSAI enforced a limit on trans fats in oils to no more than 2% in 2021.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's domestic regulatory framework and limits regarding dietary fats.
distractorHooks: The limit on trans fats in India is set at 5%
The regulation completely bans all trans fats, including natural ones, in India
The limit is enforced by the Ministry of Consumer Affairs rather than FSSAI
